Server exposes 22 tools — large attack surface without per-tool permission controls
Tool "auth_status" may expose secrets in its output based on description
Tool "auth_exchange_code" references user without specifying scope boundaries. LLM will attempt to access the broadest possible scope.
Tool "auth_status" references user without specifying scope boundaries. LLM will attempt to access the broadest possible scope.
Tool "auth_refresh_token" uses vague action words (access) without specific operations. LLMs will interpret this as the broadest possible action.
Tool "auth_logout" uses vague action words (use) without specific operations. LLMs will interpret this as the broadest possible action on sensitive resources (user).
Tool "auth_logout" references user without specifying scope boundaries. LLM will attempt to access the broadest possible scope.
Tool "get_user" references user without specifying scope boundaries. LLM will attempt to access the broadest possible scope.
Tool "update_project" uses vague action words (update) without specific operations. LLMs will interpret this as the broadest possible action.
Tool "update_task" uses vague action words (update) without specific operations. LLMs will interpret this as the broadest possible action.
Tool "search_tasks" references key without specifying scope boundaries. LLM will attempt to access the broadest possible scope.
Tools "get_tasks_due_soon" and "search_tasks" have 63% description overlap. LLM may choose between them unpredictably.
Tools "get_tasks_due_soon" and "get_high_priority_tasks" have 63% description overlap. LLM may choose between them unpredictably.
Tools "search_tasks" and "get_high_priority_tasks" have 63% description overlap. LLM may choose between them unpredictably.
